flashcards.back: A decentralized, open-source blockchain platform that enables smart contracts and decentralized applications (dApps). Proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013, launched in 2015.

flashcards.front: How does Ethereum differ from Bitcoin in its scripting capability?
flashcards.back: Bitcoin uses Bitcoin Script (limited, not Turing-complete). Ethereum supports Turing-complete programming languages (Solidity, Vyper), enabling complex smart contracts and dApps.

flashcards.front: What is the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)?
flashcards.back: A deterministic, sandboxed, stack-based, gas-metered runtime environment that executes smart contract bytecode on every Ethereum node, ensuring consistent computation across the network.

flashcards.front: What are the two types of Ethereum accounts?
flashcards.back: 1) Externally Owned Accounts (EOA): controlled by private keys, can initiate transactions, no code. 2) Contract Accounts: controlled by smart contract code, cannot initiate transactions on their own, have code and storage.
A unit of computational work. Every EVM operation has a gas cost. Users pay gas fees in ETH to compensate validators. Gas prevents infinite loops and abuse by halting execution when gas runs out.

flashcards.front: What are the ETH denominations: Wei, Gwei, and ETH?
flashcards.back: 1 ETH = 10^9 Gwei = 10^18 Wei. Wei is the smallest unit. Gwei (gigawei) is commonly used for gas prices. 1 Gwei = 10^9 Wei.

flashcards.front: What is the account model used by Ethereum?
flashcards.back: Ethereum uses an account-based model where each account has a balance and nonce (transaction counter). This differs from Bitcoin's UTXO model where balances come from unspent transaction outputs.
The transition of Ethereum from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake consensus. It merged the original execution layer with the Beacon Chain, reducing energy consumption by ~99.95%.

A self-executing program stored on the blockchain that automatically enforces agreement terms when conditions are met. It is immutable (once deployed, cannot be changed), deterministic, autonomous, and transparent.
An application with its backend running on a decentralized network (Ethereum blockchain) rather than centralized servers. Architecture: Frontend -> Web3 library -> Ethereum node -> Smart contracts.
